perturbation.original_text: Buyer acknowledges they will be conducting due diligence with regard to the Property and that such due diligence is the sole responsibility of Buyer. Buyer shall at all times conduct its due diligence review, inspections and examinations in a manner so as to not cause damage, loss, cost or expense to Owner, the Property or the tenants of the Property and so as to not interfere with or disturb any tenant at the Property, and Buyer will indemnify, protect, defend, and hold Owner and the Property harmless from and against any such breach of this Agreement. Without limitation on the foregoing, in no event shall Buyer contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s express written consent. Owner shall have the right, at its option, to cause a representative of Owner to be present at all inspections, reviews and examinations conducted hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer acknowledges they will be conducting due diligence with regard to the Property and that such due diligence is the sole responsibility of Buyer. Buyer may conduct its due diligence review, inspections and examinations in any manner they see fit, and Owner will indemnify, protect, defend, and hold Buyer harmless from and against any claims arising from such activities. Buyer is permitted to contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s consent. Owner does not have the right, at its option, to cause a representative of Owner to be present at all inspections, reviews and examinations conducted hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts several original terms. It states that the Buyer can conduct due diligence in any manner, removing the restriction against causing damage or disturbance. It reverses the indemnification obligation, shifting it from Buyer to Owner. It also allows the Buyer to contact tenants without consent, and removes the Owner's right to be present during inspections, directly contradicting and undermining the due diligence restrictions and protections for the owner.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer acknowledges they will be conducting due diligence with regard to the Property and that such due diligence is the sole responsibility of Buyer. Buyer may conduct its due diligence review, inspections and examinations in any manner they see fit, and Owner will indemnify, protect, defend, and hold Buyer harmless from and against any claims arising from such activities. Buyer is permitted to contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s consent. Owner does not have the right, at its option, to cause a representative of Owner to be present at all inspections, reviews and examinations conducted hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement by allowing the Buyer to conduct due diligence in any manner, removing restrictions on causing damage or disturbing tenants. It also shifts the indemnification obligation from the Buyer to the Owner and permits the Buyer to contact tenants without the Owner's consent, while also removing the Owner's right to be present during inspections. This directly conflicts with and undermines the due diligence restrictions and protections initially in place for the Owner as outlined in the 'Contradicted Text', which is actually just the Changed Text because it is now in the document and clearly shows the terms that strongly create an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer acknowledges that if this Agreement is breached, Owner could not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, Owner, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Buyer shall reimburse Owner for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorney’s fees, incurred by Owner if Owner successfully enforces the obligations of Buyer and its Representatives hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer acknowledges that if this Agreement is breached, Owner could be made whole by monetary damages. Owner's sole remedy for any breach of this agreement is monetary damages and shall not be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Owner will be responsible for their own legal costs.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation replaces the original text that stated that the Buyer could not be made whole with monetary damages by stating that they could. It also restricts the Owner to only monetary damages, reversing their right to seek an injunction or specific performance. This limits the original text as this section has been contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer acknowledges that if this Agreement is breached, Owner could be made whole by monetary damages. Owner's sole remedy for any breach of this agreement is monetary damages and shall not be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Owner will be responsible for their own legal costs.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent and explicitly states that the Owner is restricted to monetary damages, reversing their right to seek an injunction or specific performance, as it states in the original text. This is a clear contradiction because the changed text provides the exact same text as the contradicted text. The changed text states the buyer CAN be made whole by monetary damages, where the original text states they CANNOT. This is a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction as defined above.

perturbation.original_text: In the event the sale of the Property is consummated and the Buyer represented by a Buyer’s Broker as disclosed herein, TKG shall pay to Buyer’s Broker per a separate agreement out of the commissions received by TKG from Owner. Buyer and Buyer’s Broker agree to indemnify and hold harmless TKG and Owner, their respective affiliates, partners, subsidiaries, reasonable attorney’s fees arising out of any claim or claims by any broker, finder or similar agent (including but not limited to Broker) for commissions, fees or other 3288 Steiner Street, Suite 201, San Francisco, CA 94123 EMAIL: jgates@thekasegroup.com | TEL: 415.231.5231 | FAX: 925.406.0813 | www.thekasegroup.com compensation for bringing about any sale of the Property. Buyer shall be responsible for their broker’s confidentiality in regards to the Confidential Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event the sale of the Property is consummated, TKG shall not be obligated to pay any commission to the Buyer’s Broker. TKG and Owner agree to indemnify and hold harmless Buyer and Buyer’s Broker, their respective affiliates, partners, subsidiaries, from any claim or claims by any broker, finder or similar agent for commissions, fees, or other compensation. TKG is responsible for the brokers' confidentiality in regards to the Confidential Materials.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that TKG shall pay the Buyer's Broker a commission, while the modified version states TKG is NOT obligated to pay. This directly contradicts the first sentence. Also, the indemnification responsibility is reversed from the Buyer/Broker to TKG/Owner. The responsibilities between the Buyer and TKG, have been reversed in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event the sale of the Property is consummated, TKG shall not be obligated to pay any commission to the Buyer’s Broker. TKG and Owner agree to indemnify and hold harmless Buyer and Buyer’s Broker, their respective affiliates, partners, subsidiaries, from any claim or claims by any broker, finder or similar agent for commissions, fees, or other compensation. TKG is responsible for the brokers' confidentiality in regards to the Confidential Materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that TKG SHALL pay the Buyer's Broker a commission, while the changed version explicitly states that TKG is NOT obligated to pay. This is a direct and undeniable conflict. Furthermore, the responsibilities between the Buyer and TKG regarding indemnification and confidentiality have been reversed. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, serves to highlight the contradiction created by the modification to the original text. The explanation states that it directly contradicts the first sentence, which is true. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All of the covenants, conditions and obligations contained in this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the respective successors, permitted assigns and designees of Owner, and the successors of Buyer. Buyer may not assign this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All of the covenants, conditions and obligations contained in this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the respective successors, permitted assigns and designees of Owner, and the successors of Buyer. Buyer may assign this Agreement without Owner's consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the buyer may NOT assign the agreement, this modification directly contradicts this by saying the Buyer CAN assign the agreement without consent. This creates an internal contradiction in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All of the covenants, conditions and obligations contained in this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the respective successors, permitted assigns and designees of Owner, and the successors of Buyer. Buyer may assign this Agreement without Owner's consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Buyer 'may not assign this agreement'. The changed text states that the Buyer 'may assign this Agreement without Owner's consent'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, thus creating a direct contradiction, as one states an action cannot occur and the other states it can. This is a clear and direct contradiction based on the explanation, and the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Entire Agreement: Modification. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties concerning its subject matter. There are no promises or other agreements, oral or written, express or implied, between them other than as set forth in this Agreement concerning the subject matter of this Agreement. No change or modification of, or waiver under, this Agreement shall be valid unless it is in writing and signed by duly authorized representatives of Owner and Buyer.
perturbation.changed_text: Entire Agreement: Modification. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties concerning its subject matter. There are no promises or other agreements, oral or written, express or implied, between them other than as set forth in this Agreement concerning the subject matter of this Agreement. Changes or modifications can be made orally and do not require both parties' signatures.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states modifications MUST be in writing, and signed by both parties. This modified text directly contradicts that by saying changes can be made orally and don't require signatures, directly contradicting it and thus creating an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement: Modification. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties concerning its subject matter. There are no promises or other agreements, oral or written, express or implied, between them other than as set forth in this Agreement concerning the subject matter of this Agreement. Changes or modifications can be made orally and do not require both parties' signatures.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that any modifications to the agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that modifications can be made orally and do not require both parties' signatures. This creates a clear and direct conflict, as evidenced by the perturbation explanation, making the original terms regarding modification unenforceable. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which highlights the directness of the conflict. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
